src/chars.ts.summary: Declares the `Chars` const enum: human-readable names for character code points (ASCII, whitespace, surrogates, BMP bounds) used across the lexer.
src/chars.ts.outputs: Exports the `Chars` const enum of numeric code-point constants.
src/chars.ts.should: Serve as the single source of named character-code constants for lexer modules.
src/chars.ts.shouldNot: Contain runtime logic, functions, or imports; know about tokens, parsing, or AST.

src/common.ts.summary: Shared parser enums (Context, PropertyKind, BindingKind, Origin, AssignmentKind, DestructuringKind, Flags, Hoisted*) plus stateless helpers on the Parser: token consume/optional, binding/function-name validation, label checks, pattern reinterpretation, identifier classification, and the `Location` type.
src/common.ts.inputs: Parser instance, Context masks, Tokens, BindingKind, AST nodes, label maps.
src/common.ts.outputs: Enums, `Location` type; helpers returning void/0-1/booleans/mutated nodes.
src/common.ts.should: Provide reusable bit-mask flags and small validation/consumption utilities depending only on Parser, token, errors, and lexer scan.
src/common.ts.shouldNot: Implement full statement/expression parsing or touch the compiler/AST-producing grammar; import from parser.ts.
src/common.ts.side: Advances tokens via nextToken; throws through parser.report; mutates node types in reinterpretToPattern; sets parser flags/destructible.

src/errors.ts.summary: Defines the `Errors` const enum, an error-message template table, and the `ParseError` class (extends SyntaxError) that formats messages with line/column ranges and %-param substitution.
src/errors.ts.inputs: Error type, start/end Location, substitution params.
src/errors.ts.should: Centralize all diagnostic message text and error object construction.
src/errors.ts.shouldNot: Decide when errors occur or perform any lexing/parsing control flow.
src/errors.ts.side: ParseError construction only (no throwing here); pure data otherwise.

src/estree.ts.summary: Type-only declarations of the ESTree AST: Node union, statement/expression/declaration/pattern interfaces, JSX nodes, Comment, SourceLocation, Position.
src/estree.ts.outputs: Exported TypeScript interfaces and type aliases for the produced AST.
src/estree.ts.should: Describe the shape of nodes the parser emits and consumers read.
src/estree.ts.shouldNot: Contain runtime code, values, or logic.

src/meriyah.ts.summary: Public library entry point: `parse`, and deprecated `parseScript`/`parseModule` wrappers delegating to parseSource, plus `version` and re-exported `Options` and `ESTree` types.
src/meriyah.ts.outputs: ESTree.Program; exported version string and types.
src/meriyah.ts.should: Offer the stable top-level API and forward to the internal parser.
src/meriyah.ts.shouldNot: Implement parsing or lexing logic itself.
src/meriyah.ts.side: Delegates to parseSource (which throws on invalid input).

src/options.ts.summary: Declares the `Options` interface (sourceType, jsx, ranges, loc, raw, webcompat, onComment/onToken callbacks, deprecated module/globalReturn), NormalizedOptions, callback types, and `normalizeOptions` which applies defaults and legacy flag mapping.
src/options.ts.outputs: NormalizedOptions with validateRegex defaulted and sourceType inferred from deprecated flags.
src/options.ts.should: Define parser configuration and normalize deprecated fields.
src/options.ts.shouldNot: Perform parsing/lexing or validate source content.

src/parser.ts.summary: The recursive-descent parser: `parseSource` plus the full grammar of parse* functions producing the ESTree AST from tokens, handling statements, expressions, classes, modules, JSX, destructuring, scopes, and strict/module semantics.
src/parser.ts.inputs: Source string, Options, initial Context; drives a Parser and its lexer.
src/parser.ts.should: Orchestrate tokenization into a spec-compliant AST, enforcing syntax rules and reporting errors.
src/parser.ts.shouldNot: Reimplement low-level character scanning; own error-message text or node type declarations.
src/parser.ts.side: Mutates Parser state, advances tokens, registers scope bindings, invokes onComment/onToken, throws on syntax errors.

src/token.ts.summary: Defines the `Token` const enum as a bit-packed set of token kinds/attribute flags/precedence, plus `KeywordDescTable` (index→text) and `descKeywordTable` (keyword string→Token).
src/token.ts.outputs: `Token` enum and the two lookup tables.
src/token.ts.should: Encode token identity, categories, and precedence for lexer and parser to test via bit masks.
src/token.ts.shouldNot: Contain scanning/parsing logic or mutable state.

src/unicode.ts.summary: Decompresses an embedded Unicode v17 table into a Uint32Array bitmap and exposes `isIDStart`/`isIDContinue` predicates for non-ASCII identifier code points.
src/unicode.ts.outputs: Boolean whether the code point is an ID_Start / ID_Continue character.
src/unicode.ts.should: Provide fast Unicode identifier classification via the precomputed lookup.
src/unicode.ts.shouldNot: Handle ASCII fast-paths (done in charClassifier) or token/parse logic.
src/unicode.ts.side: Builds the lookup table once at module load (module-level computation).

src/utilities.ts.summary: Single helper `getOwnProperty` returning an own property value or undefined, avoiding prototype-chain lookups on record maps.
src/utilities.ts.inputs: A record object and a string/number key.
src/utilities.ts.outputs: The own property value or undefined.
src/utilities.ts.should: Provide safe own-property access for lookup tables.
src/utilities.ts.shouldNot: Grow into a general utility grab-bag or depend on parser internals.

src/lexer/charClassifier.ts.summary: Defines `CharFlags`, the ASCII `CharTypes` flag table, `isIdStart`/`isIdPart` boolean arrays, and `isIdentifierStart`/`isIdentifierPart` predicates (ASCII table fast path, delegating to unicode.ts above 0x7F).
src/lexer/charClassifier.ts.outputs: Flag lookups and identifier-start/part boolean predicates.
src/lexer/charClassifier.ts.should: Classify characters for the lexer, combining ASCII tables with Unicode fallbacks.
src/lexer/charClassifier.ts.shouldNot: Scan tokens or mutate parser state.

src/lexer/comments.ts.summary: Comment scanning: `skipHashBang`, `skipSingleLineComment`, `skipSingleHTMLComment`, `skipMultiLineComment`, and the `CommentType` enum; advances past comments, tracks newlines, and fires onComment.
src/lexer/comments.ts.inputs: Parser, source string, LexerState, comment type, start Location, Context.
src/lexer/comments.ts.outputs: Updated LexerState (newline flags); reports errors for unterminated/module HTML comments.
src/lexer/comments.ts.should: Consume comment text and invoke the onComment callback with correct spans.
src/lexer/comments.ts.side: Advances parser index/line/column, sets NewLine flags, calls onComment, throws on unterminated comment.

src/lexer/common.ts.outputs: Advanced char codes, merged surrogate code points, hex values, whitespace booleans, token-type strings.
src/lexer/common.ts.should: Provide shared char-stream cursor movement and small conversions for all scanners.
src/lexer/common.ts.side: advanceChar/scanNewLine/consumeLineFeed mutate parser index/column/line/currentChar/flags; others pure.

src/lexer/decodeHTML.ts.summary: Holds the HTML named-entity and legacy numeric-code maps and `decodeHTMLStrict`, which replaces `&entity;`/numeric references in JSX text with their characters.
src/lexer/decodeHTML.ts.outputs: The string with HTML entities decoded (invalid code points → U+FFFD).
src/lexer/decodeHTML.ts.should: Decode HTML entities for JSX text tokens.

src/lexer/identifier.ts.summary: Identifier scanning: `scanIdentifier`, `scanUnicodeIdentifier`, `scanIdentifierSlowCase` (handling backslash-u escapes and surrogate pairs), `scanPrivateIdentifier`, and internal unicode-escape decoders; resolves keyword vs identifier and escaped-keyword tokens.
src/lexer/identifier.ts.should: Read identifier characters, apply keyword table, and enforce escaped-keyword rules.
src/lexer/identifier.ts.shouldNot: Handle non-identifier tokens or parse expressions.
src/lexer/identifier.ts.side: Advances parser cursor, sets tokenValue/currentChar, throws on invalid escapes/chars.

src/lexer/index.ts.summary: Barrel module re-exporting lexer entry points: scanSingleToken/nextToken, skipHashBang, and common helpers (advanceChar, consumeLineFeed, scanNewLine, LexerState, convertTokenType).
src/lexer/index.ts.should: Give the parser a single import surface for the lexer.

src/lexer/jsx.ts.summary: JSX-specific scanning: `scanJSXAttributeValue`, `scanJSXString`, `nextJSXToken` (JSXText with HTML-entity decoding), and `rescanJSXIdentifier` (allowing hyphens).
src/lexer/jsx.ts.should: Tokenize JSX text, strings, and hyphenated identifiers distinctly from normal JS scanning.
src/lexer/jsx.ts.shouldNot: Parse JSX grammar/build nodes or handle non-JSX tokens.
src/lexer/jsx.ts.side: Advances parser cursor, sets token state, decodes entities, throws on unterminated string/empty text.

src/lexer/numeric.ts.summary: `scanNumber` scans decimal/float/hex/octal/binary/implicit-octal/BigInt literals with numeric-separator and strict-mode handling; helper `scanDecimalDigitsOrSeparator` collects digits skipping underscores.
src/lexer/numeric.ts.outputs: Token.NumericLiteral or BigIntLiteral; sets tokenValue (number/bigint) and tokenRaw.
src/lexer/numeric.ts.should: Parse all numeric literal forms and enforce separator/strict/identifier-after-number rules.
src/lexer/numeric.ts.side: Advances parser cursor, sets tokenValue/tokenRaw/flags, throws on malformed literals.

src/lexer/regexp.ts.summary: `scanRegularExpression` scans a regex body and flags (checking duplicates/validity), and `validate` compiles it via RegExp, returning the RegExp, null, or reporting per options.validateRegex.
src/lexer/regexp.ts.should: Tokenize regular-expression literals and optionally validate them.
src/lexer/regexp.ts.shouldNot: Parse regex internals beyond flag checks or handle other tokens.
src/lexer/regexp.ts.side: Advances parser cursor, sets token state, throws on unterminated/duplicate/invalid regex.

src/lexer/scan.ts.summary: Core tokenizer: `nextToken` and `scanSingleToken` with the ASCII `TokenLookup` table; dispatches whitespace, comments, punctuators/operators (multi-char), and delegates to the specialized identifier/number/string/template/regex scanners.
src/lexer/scan.ts.outputs: The next Token; updates parser token/flags/position via setToken.
src/lexer/scan.ts.should: Drive the main scan loop and route each start character to the right scanner.
src/lexer/scan.ts.shouldNot: Implement each literal's detailed scanning (delegated) or parse grammar.
src/lexer/scan.ts.side: Mutates parser position/flags/token, skips comments/whitespace, throws on illegal characters.

src/lexer/string.ts.summary: `scanString` scans string literals; `parseEscape` decodes escape sequences (octal, hex, \u, \u{}, line continuations, 8/9) returning a code point or negative Escape code; `handleStringError` maps Escape codes to reported errors.
src/lexer/string.ts.inputs: Parser, Context, quote/first char, isTemplate flag.
src/lexer/string.ts.outputs: Token.StringLiteral; escape code points or Escape sentinels; sets tokenValue/tokenRaw.
src/lexer/string.ts.should: Read string bodies and decode/validate escape sequences with strict/webcompat rules.
src/lexer/string.ts.shouldNot: Scan templates directly (though shared by template.ts) or other tokens.
src/lexer/string.ts.side: Advances parser cursor, sets tokenValue/flags, throws on invalid/unterminated strings.

src/lexer/template.ts.summary: `scanTemplate` scans template spans/continuations with escape handling and tagged-template tolerance for bad escapes (via `scanBadTemplate`); `scanTemplateTail` resumes after `}`.
src/lexer/template.ts.outputs: Token.TemplateSpan or TemplateContinuation; sets tokenValue (cooked, or null) and tokenRaw.
src/lexer/template.ts.should: Tokenize template literal sections, reusing string escape logic.
src/lexer/template.ts.shouldNot: Parse template-embedded expressions or other token kinds.
src/lexer/template.ts.side: Advances parser cursor, sets token state, throws on unterminated template.

src/parser/parser.ts.summary: The `Parser` class holding mutable lex/parse state (position, current token, flags, exported names, scopes) plus methods: token get/set with onToken buffering, `finishNode` (ranges/loc), `report`, scope/private-scope factories, node cloning, and comment/token push adapters.
src/parser/parser.ts.inputs: Source string and raw Options; various state mutations from lexer/parser.
src/parser/parser.ts.outputs: Parser state, finished nodes with location info, ParseError throws, Scope/PrivateScope instances.
src/parser/parser.ts.should: Own parser/lexer state and provide node-finishing, error, and scope construction services.
src/parser/parser.ts.shouldNot: Contain grammar rules or character scanning.
src/parser/parser.ts.side: Mutable instance state; invokes onToken/onComment; throws via report.

src/parser/private-scope.ts.summary: `PrivateScope` class tracking private-identifier declarations and references per class body, enforcing duplicate/getter-setter/static rules and validating unresolved references.
src/parser/private-scope.ts.inputs: Parser, parent scope, private name, PropertyKind.
src/parser/private-scope.ts.should: Model private-name lexical scoping and report duplicate/undefined private identifiers.
src/parser/private-scope.ts.shouldNot: Track ordinary variable bindings (that is Scope) or parse class bodies.
src/parser/private-scope.ts.side: Mutates its maps/refs; reports duplicate errors; throws on undefined private refs during validation.

src/parser/scope.ts.summary: `Scope` class and `ScopeKind` enum implementing lexical/var binding tracking with duplicate-binding, webcompat, catch-shadowing, and function-body redeclaration rules; plus `createArrowHeadParsingScope` helper.
src/parser/scope.ts.inputs: Parser, Context, binding name, BindingKind, Origin, ScopeKind, parent scope.
src/parser/scope.ts.outputs: Scope instances with variableBindings maps; recorded/thrown binding errors.
src/parser/scope.ts.should: Model variable/block scoping and enforce binding-collision rules.
src/parser/scope.ts.shouldNot: Handle private identifiers (that is PrivateScope) or drive parsing.
src/parser/scope.ts.side: Mutates binding maps and scopeError; reports/throws duplicate-binding and shadowed-catch errors.
